q: What is the primary function of a camera as an optical instrument?
a: forms and records an image of an object

q: How may the image in a camera be recorded?
a: on film (or) it may be detected by an electronic sensor

q: What type of image does a convex lens in a camera form on the film or electronic sensor?
What is the purpose of the diaphragm in a camera?
a: to control the amount of light energy falling on the film
to adjust the focal length of the lens

q: Where is the diaphragm placed in a simple camera?
a: in front of the convex lens

q: What is the aperture in a camera?
a: a circular opening through which the light passes
the main lens of the camera

q: How is the aperture in a camera made wide or narrow?
In what terms is the size or diameter of the aperture expressed?
What is the f-number of a camera defined as?
a: the ratio of the focal length of the lens to the diameter of aperture
the ratio of exposure time to shutter speed
the magnification power of the lens

q: What does a smaller f-number indicate about the aperture?
a: the shorter the focal length is

q: When taking a photograph in bright light, what type of f-number and aperture should be used?
What mechanism controls the time during which light enters the camera through the lens?
How is the shutter speed expressed in a camera?
a: in terms of the exposure time

q: What relationship exists between a shorter exposure time and shutter speed?
a: the higher the shutter speed is
the lower the shutter speed is
the exposure time is unrelated to shutter speed

q: What is the function of the focusing ring in a camera?
a: to adjust the distance between the lens and the film to obtain a sharp image
to control the amount of light

q: What is the film at the back of the camera described as?
a: a screen which receives the image

q: What does the film consist of?
a: a thin layer of chemical compounds of silver

q: What process occurs when light energy falls on the film?
In a digital camera, what replaces the film?
What is an electronic sensor in a digital camera made up of?
a: millions of tiny pixels which response to light
